title: Energy-Charts Price API Response
description: Response format from Energy-Charts price endpoint for Dutch electricity day-ahead prices

license_info.description: Licensing and data attribution information. Indicates data is CC BY 4.0 licensed from Bundesnetzagentur/SMARD.de

unix_seconds.description: Array of Unix timestamps (seconds since epoch) in UTC timezone. Each timestamp represents the start of a 15-minute interval. Must have same length as price array.

items.description: Unix timestamp in seconds (UTC). Increments by 900 seconds (15 minutes) between consecutive elements.

price.description: Array of day-ahead electricity prices corresponding to each timestamp. Each price aligns with the unix_seconds array at the same index. Prices are from the SMARD.de day-ahead auction.

items.description: Price in EUR/MWh (Euro per Megawatt-hour). Can be positive (normal), zero (rare), or negative (very rare, oversupply).

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the price values

deprecated.description: Flag indicating whether this API endpoint or response format is deprecated. Monitor for API migration planning.

notes.array_length: Both unix_seconds and price arrays must have identical length
notes.timezone: All timestamps are in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time)
notes.data_source: SMARD.de - German day-ahead electricity market data
notes.price_range: Typically 0-200+ EUR/MWh, but can vary based on market conditions
notes.missing_values: Rare; null values may appear but should be handled as 0 or excluded
notes.timestamp_conversion: Use datetime.fromtimestamp(unix_ts, tz=timezone.utc) to convert to ISO 8601 format
